data.description: Aspo Plc buys back its shares on 20.1.2026.

data.title: PenFed Foundation and FFVC partner in Raleigh to support veteran entrepreneurs; program equips early-stage businesses.
data.description: Partnering with First Flight Venture Center (FFVC), the Raleigh program of the Foundation aims to support veteran entrepreneurs in the early stages of their ventures. This initiative, based in Raleigh, North Carolina, was announced on January 20, 2026, and intends to provide essential resources to enable aspiring entrepreneurs to realize their business concepts. Through this collaboration, facilitated by the PenFed Foundation for Military Heroes, veterans will gain access to the tools and support necessary for successful business development.

data.title: Rokt partners with Cineplex to use AI in ecommerce checkout, adding non-endemic offers, boosting revenue.
data.description: Cineplex has announced plans to incorporate non-endemic offers into its checkout process, enhancing customer benefits and creating additional revenue sources. This move comes as part of the company's efforts to diversify its services and increase profitability. This partnership, set to commence on January 20, 2026, with Rokt, a prominent ecommerce technology firm leveraging machine learning and AI for optimized transactions. The integration aims to streamline the customer experience while exploring new avenues for financial growth.

data.title: Secretary Bessent advises Powell against attending Supreme Court argument on Fed's independence after he himself attended a tariff case.
data.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticized Jerome Powell for attending hearings on Fed independence, commenting that it was a misstep. Interestingly, Bessent himself participated in a tariff case.

data.title: Certified Laboratories becomes Amazon-approved TIC for supplements, solidifying their position in regulated product testing.
data.description: Certified Laboratories, a company within the Certified Group based in Burbank, California, has further established its position as a leader in regulated product testing. On January 20, 2026, they announced their official recognition as an Amazon-approved Testing, Inspection, and Certification (TIC) organization for dietary products. This expansion solidifies their expertise in this sector and highlights their commitment to meeting industry standards. This recognition aligns with their goal of ensuring quality and safety, showcasing their dedication to excellence in the field.

French President Emmanuel Macron surprised delegates at the World Economic Forum in Davos by arriving in fetching sunglasses and wearing them during his keynote speech. Macron denounced US "competition" by counterpart Donald Trump which aims to "weaken and subordinate Europe".

data.title: Juniper Biomedical wins Phase 3 of NIH SPARC Neuromod Prize; Neuropace founder Frank Fischer joins Board of Directors.
data.description: A company based in Worcester, Massachusetts called Juniper Biomedical has recently announced the addition of Frank Fischer, the founder of Neuropace, to its Board of Directors. Additionally, the company has achieved success by winning Phase 3 of the NIH SPARC Neuromod Prize. This achievement marks a significant milestone for Juniper Biomedical in the field of neuromodulation.

data.title: Global Induction Motors market to see 25.2% CAGR, valued at USD 1030.8M in 2023, expected to reach USD 5098.4M by 2030.
data.description: In Bangalore, India, as of January 20th, 2026, the current market value of Induction Motors stands at USD 1030.8 million, as reported by PRNewswire. Forecasts suggest that this market is expected to soar to USD 5098.4 million by the year 2030. This growth represents a significant Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 25.2% projected for the period spanning from 2024 to 2030.
